_comment: UMich-ITS Chatbot - Troubleshooting flows (v1). Top 3 intents only: VPN, MPrint, Okta. Designed to pair with intents.json + synonyms.json + questions.json.
_comment_design: Conversation flow: (1) normalize with synonyms.json, (2) score intents.json, (3) ask universal questions, (4) ask 2 intent-specific questions, (5) select a flow and print steps.
This file is intentionally procedural + non-ML. It is safe to extend with more intents later.
Do NOT ask the user to paste passwords. Allow them to authenticate only via official login prompts.

official_links._comment: Keep URLs here so you can reuse them in multiple flows.

escalation._comment: You can replace these with the exact campus contacts you want to display.
escalation.generic_message: If these steps don’t fix it, contact the ITS Service Center and include: device type, OS, time of attempt, location (building/room), network used, and the exact error message.

vpn_remote_access._comment: Uses questions.json keys: vpn_client, vpn_offcampus, location, connection_type. Also useful: device_scope, recent_change.

entry.title: VPN / Remote Access
when_to_use: User mentions VPN, UMVPN, Cisco AnyConnect, Cisco Secure Client, off-campus access, remote desktop blocked
entry.safety_note: Do not request passwords. Only direct the user to official login prompts.

install_and_connect.title: Install and connect to U-M Net VPN (UMVPN)
steps: Confirm you have an active internet connection first (WiFi/Ethernet/Hotspot).
Install the VPN client using the official Getting Started guide (choose your OS).
Open Cisco Secure Client (or AnyConnect, if that’s what the user has).
Select the profile: 'UMVPN - All Traffic' or 'UMVPN - Only U-M Traffic'.
Click Connect.
Authenticate with uniqname + UMICH password when prompted.
Complete MFA when prompted.
notes: You must turn ON VPN before opening services that require it.
Alumni, retirees, and friend accounts generally cannot use UMVPN (eligibility rules apply).
escalate_if: User cannot install due to admin restrictions
User is not eligible for VPN but needs access
MFA enrollment/login is failing (route to Okta intent)

eligibility_check.title: Check if you’re eligible for U-M VPN
steps: If you are a current student, faculty, staff, or sponsored affiliate with a regular uniqname, you should be eligible while that affiliation is active.
If you are alumni/retiree/friend account or have a temporary uniqname, VPN access may be blocked.
If eligibility is unclear, escalate with your affiliation type and uniqname category.
escalate_if: User believes they should be eligible but VPN access is blocked

choose_profile.title: Which UMVPN profile should I use?
steps: Use 'UMVPN - All Traffic' when you need all internet traffic routed through U-M (also helps with geo-blocked services).
Use 'UMVPN - Only U-M Traffic' when you only need U-M resources routed through VPN.
If a form/site is blocked by geographic restrictions, try 'All Traffic'.

miworkspace_note.title: MiWorkspace managed device note
steps: On some MiWorkspace Windows/PaaS devices, the Cisco Management Tunnel may be used automatically for standard UMVPN behavior.
If you still need to manually connect via Cisco Secure Client, use the UMVPN profile options available to you.
escalate_if: User needs remote desktop off-campus and is blocked even with VPN on

temp_folder_error_windows.title: Windows: 'Failed to determine valid temporary file folder'
steps: This usually happens when the Cisco temp folder is missing.
Go to C:\Windows\Temp\
Create a folder named 'Cisco'.
Try connecting again.
escalate_if: User cannot create folders due to permissions
Error persists after folder creation

linux_libxml2_issue.title: Linux: Cisco Secure Client libxml2 compatibility issue
steps: This can occur after a libxml2 update where the client expects a missing library version.
Option A: downgrade libxml2 to a compatible version for your distribution.
Option B: place a compatible libxml2 library locally and adjust LD_LIBRARY_PATH for the client.
Option C: update Cisco Secure Client to the latest available version.
notes: Linux support may be limited; changes to system libraries should be done carefully.
escalate_if: User is uncomfortable changing system libraries
Client fails after updates and needs official support

slow_or_bad_request_app.title: Site loads slowly / bad request (network-related) — try VPN or different network
steps: Try loading the site on a different network (switch WiFi networks or try mobile data).
If eligible, connect to UMVPN and try again.
If using a bookmark, type the URL directly or refresh the bookmark.
escalate_if: Issue happens on multiple networks + VPN
Only one specific service is failing consistently

printing_mprint_setup._comment: Uses questions.json keys: print_environment, print_symptom, location, connection_type.

entry.title: Printing / MPrint Setup
when_to_use: User mentions MPrint, PaperCut, Follow Me, print queue, release, campus printer
requirements: To install printers AND to print: user must be on campus Ethernet, on MWireless, or connected to UMVPN
notes: Campus Computing Sites printers are not available for installation; use Follow Me, mprint.umich.edu, or a Campus Computing Sites computer.
Unit-managed devices (e.g., Michigan Medicine/CoreImage) should contact their unit IT.
entry.safety_note: Do not ask for passwords. Only direct them to official login prompts.

default.title: Install MPrint Printer Installer and add printers / Follow Me
steps: Confirm network access: on-campus Ethernet, MWireless, or UMVPN (required to install and print).
Go to mprint.umich.edu/install and sign in if prompted.
Download the MPrint Installer application (link in the informational box).
Run the downloaded installer to install the MPrint Printer Installer.
Open the MPrint Printer Installer (Desktop shortcut on Windows, Applications folder on macOS).
On first launch, log in when prompted, then return to the application.
Use Quick Search or MPrint Maps to find queues, then install the printer queue(s) you need.
Optional: install the 'Follow Me' queue so you can release at any compatible printer.

cant_find_printer.title: Can’t find an MPrint printer
steps: Confirm you are connected to campus Ethernet/MWireless or UMVPN.
Open the MPrint Printer Installer and sign in if prompted.
Use Quick Search (top right) with the building/printer name.
Try MPrint Maps and choose the building + floor, then install from the map.
If it’s a Campus Computing Sites printer: you cannot install it directly—use Follow Me, mprint.umich.edu, or a Campus Computing Sites computer.

wont_print.title: Print job won’t print
steps: Confirm you are connected to campus Ethernet/MWireless or UMVPN.
Try printing to the Follow Me queue instead of a specific device.
If prompted by PaperCut, respond within 10 minutes or the job may be deleted.
If PaperCut prompts for credentials, enter uniqname + UMICH password and select 'Remember my identity' if appropriate.
If PaperCut didn’t launch properly, log out and log back in, then try again.

stuck_in_queue.title: Print job stuck in queue / not releasing
steps: If you printed to Follow Me: release at a printer (Mcard swipe / printer login / MPrint Mobile app).
You can also release or cancel jobs via mprint.umich.edu/release.
If the job fails due to a jam or parse error and cannot resume, resubmit the job to Follow Me and release again.

cant_release.title: Can’t release Follow Me job
steps: Try releasing via mprint.umich.edu/release (release or cancel from the site).
Try releasing at the printer again (Mcard swipe, printer login, or MPrint Mobile app).
If the job failed due to jam/parse error, re-print to Follow Me and release again.
Log out of the printer when you’re done.

papercut_saved_identity_wrong.title: PaperCut prompting / wrong saved identity
steps: Open the PaperCut client configuration file:
Windows: C:\Users\[uniqname]\pc-client.properties
macOS: /Users/[username]/Library/Preferences/PCClient/config.properties
Find 'neverrequestidentity' and change it so PaperCut will ask again (set to N).
Linux: delete ~/pc-client.properties and restart PaperCut.
Restart PaperCut (or log out/in) and enter the correct uniqname when prompted.

windows_security_or_admin.title: Windows blocked installer / needs admin
steps: If you see 'Windows protected your PC': right-click installer > Properties > Unblock (if present) > run again.
If the MPrint Installer says it must run as Administrator: close it, then right-click the MPrint Installer shortcut and choose 'Run as Administrator'.
Launch the installed 'MPrint Installer' shortcut (not the setup EXE again).
If no shortcut exists: check Start Menu or open %localappdata%\mprint_installer in File Explorer.
escalate_if: User does not have admin rights

bitdefender_conflict.title: BitDefender blocks MPrint install (personal Windows)
steps: Temporarily disable BitDefender.
Run the MPrint Installer and complete initial setup (including PaperCut).
Re-enable BitDefender after setup completes.

mobile_upload_ios.title: Upload/Print to Follow Me from iPhone (iOS)
steps: Install and open the MPrint app.
Log in with uniqname + UMICH password (stay logged in).
Export/Share the document as a PDF.
Choose 'MPrint Follow Me' from the share/open menu.
Tap Print.
Release via the mobile app (nearby printer list or scan printer QR code).

mobile_release_android.title: Release Follow Me from Android
steps: Open the MPrint mobile app and go to Select Jobs.
Select job(s) to release (you can also delete/cancel).
Choose a printer from nearby list or scan the printer QR code.
Release the selected job(s).

duo_okta_authentication._comment: Okta replaced Duo. This intent handles enrollment, sign-in method selection, Verify app issues, and FastPass. Uses questions.json keys: duo_symptom, duo_method (you may rename later to okta_symptom/okta_method).

entry.title: Okta Authentication (MFA / Enrollment / FastPass)
when_to_use: User mentions Okta, Okta Verify, MFA, 2FA, push, FastPass, enrollment, stuck loading, looping login
entry.safety_note: Do not ask for passwords. Only direct the user to official login prompts.

first_time_enroll_computer_plus_phone.title: First-time enrollment (best experience: computer + phone)
steps: On a computer, go to a U-M sign-in page (e.g., Wolverine Access) and enter uniqname/email, then UMICH password.
When prompted to set up MFA, proceed to set up Okta Verify.
On your phone, install the 'Okta Verify' app from the official app store.
In Okta Verify: Get Started → Add Account → Organization → enter/select the U-M org when prompted (okta.umich.edu), then scan the QR code shown on the computer.
If you can’t scan a QR code, choose the 'Can’t scan?' option and use the SMS verification path if offered.
Finish setup and click Done/Continue until you reach confirmation.
notes: If any app asks for payment, it’s the wrong app (Okta Verify is free).
Consider adding a backup MFA method/device later if supported.
escalate_if: User cannot receive SMS (or SMS not supported for their number)
Enrollment fails repeatedly or QR never works

enroll_mobile_only.title: Enroll using only a mobile device (no computer)
steps: On your phone, open the sign-in page and enter uniqname/email and UMICH password.
Proceed to enrollment and install Okta Verify if prompted.
If the process sends you an SMS link, verify it points to the official U-M Okta domain before opening.
Complete the Okta Verify setup inside the app (biometric optional).
Finish and confirm enrollment is complete.
escalate_if: User cannot receive SMS
User has a new phone and no backup method

choose_different_mfa_method.title: Choose a different MFA method at sign-in
steps: On the Okta sign-in page, enter uniqname (if needed) and password (if needed).
If your default method doesn’t appear, select an MFA option from the list.
Use 'Verify with something else' (or similar) to switch methods.
If push is undesirable or failing, switch to 'Enter a code' if available.
notes: If a desktop FastPass option is taking over, fully quit the desktop Okta Verify app to use other methods.

touch_id_stopped_working_ios.title: iOS: Touch ID stopped working with Okta Verify
steps: Set up Touch ID on the iPhone/iPad again in device settings.
Force-quit Okta Verify and reopen it.
If you see an out-of-sync message, quit and reopen the Okta Verify app again.

setup_fastpass_windows.title: Set up Okta FastPass (Windows)
steps: Enroll in Okta on a mobile device first (Okta Verify or a supported token method).
Ensure Windows Hello is configured (PIN/biometric).
Open Okta Verify on Windows (MiWorkspace may already have it installed).
Get Started → Next → enter organization URL if needed: https://okta.umich.edu
Sign in and approve the prompt (push / verification).
Enable Windows Hello when prompted.
Test FastPass in a private/incognito window using: https://oktaverify.umich.edu/verify
notes: Browsers may ask permission to discover devices on local network; Allow is typically required for FastPass to work.

setup_fastpass_macos.title: Set up Okta FastPass (macOS)
steps: Enroll in Okta on a mobile device first (Okta Verify or a supported token method).
Ensure Touch ID is enabled (if you want Touch ID-based FastPass).
Open Okta Verify on the Mac (MiWorkspace may already have it installed).
Get Started → Next → enter organization URL if needed: https://okta.umich.edu
Sign in and approve the prompt (push / verification).
Enable Touch ID if prompted.
Test FastPass in a private window using: https://oktaverify.umich.edu/verify
notes: If you don’t see the Okta Verify icon in the menu bar, you may have too many menu bar items; close some.

flows._comment_general_no_docs: The next flows are general best-practice steps when you don't have official docs for the exact symptom. They are safe, non-destructive, and designed to reduce false escalations.

push_not_arriving_general.title: Okta Verify push not arriving (general steps)
steps: Confirm the phone has internet access (WiFi or cellular data).
Open the Okta Verify app directly (don’t wait only for a notification).
Force-quit Okta Verify and reopen it.
Toggle airplane mode ON then OFF (resets network).
If possible, switch networks (WiFi ↔ cellular) and try again.
At the sign-in screen, choose 'Verify with something else' and use 'Enter a code' if available.
escalate_if: Push never arrives across networks
User has no alternative MFA method configured

stuck_loading_or_loop_general.title: Okta sign-in stuck loading or looping (general steps)
steps: Try a private/incognito browser window.
Clear cookies/site data for the sign-in domain and try again.
Try a different browser (Chrome ↔ Edge ↔ Safari).
Disable browser extensions temporarily (especially ad-blockers/privacy tools).
If on a managed workstation with Okta desktop FastPass, fully quit the desktop Okta Verify app and retry selecting another MFA method.
escalate_if: Loop persists on multiple browsers + incognito
User cannot reach the MFA selection page

new_phone_no_backup_general.title: New phone / lost device and can’t approve MFA (general steps)
steps: If you have another enrolled device (tablet/desktop FastPass/token), use it to sign in and add the new phone in Okta Verify.
If you do NOT have a backup MFA method/device, you will likely need account assistance to transfer/reactivate MFA.
Escalate to ITS and state: 'new phone, no backup MFA device' + your affiliation + any error text.
escalate_if: User has no backup method
